skill.name: cc-skill-project-guidelines-example
skill.description: Provides example project-specific guidelines for a Next.js, FastAPI, Supabase, and Claude API application.
skill.summary: Use a project guideline template for architecture, code patterns, testing, and deployment.
skill.author: affaan-m

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged shell commands, network calls, environment variables, and weak crypto patterns in SKILL.md. Manual review found these are documentation examples and placeholders, not executable skill code or credential access. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or confirmed malicious behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Documentation Shell Examples Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: The command-related detections occur inside Markdown fences, diagrams, and example snippets. They are not executable skill logic and do not create command injection risk by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is Markdown guidance, and the cited lines are example test and deploy commands. No script file or dynamic command construction was found.

low_findings.title: Network Examples Do Not Exfiltrate Data
low_findings.description: The fetch example uses a relative API path, and the URLs are service examples or documentation references. No evidence shows outbound transmission of secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network patterns are documentation examples and placeholder service URLs. I found no code path that sends credentials to an external endpoint.

low_findings.title: Placeholder Environment Variables Are Not Secret Access
low_findings.description: The environment variable section lists expected configuration names and placeholder values. It does not read local files, access process environments, or expose real credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are a Markdown configuration example with redacted placeholder values. No actual environment file read or runtime secret handling exists in the skill.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Semantic False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines do not define cryptographic algorithms or security-sensitive hashing. They are ordinary project description and example schema text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar weak cryptographic use at these locations. The detector appears to have matched substrings in non-security text.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose consistency when architecture, testing, and deployment rules live in separate places. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a concise project guideline template.
actual_capabilities: Summarizes a full-stack project architecture for AI coding assistants.
Provides example file organization for frontend, backend, deployment, and documentation.
Defines reusable API response, frontend fetch, Claude integration, and React hook patterns.
Lists backend, frontend, and end-to-end testing commands and expectations.
Documents deployment checklist items and required environment variables.
States project rules for TDD, validation, coverage, and error handling.
limitations: It is an example template, not a complete project policy.
It does not include runnable automation or validation scripts.
It references placeholder service URLs and placeholder secret names.
It assumes a Next.js, FastAPI, Supabase, and Cloud Run stack.

use_cases.title: Standardize a New Project
use_cases.description: Create a shared baseline for architecture, folders, test commands, and release checks.

use_cases.title: Guide AI Coding Sessions
use_cases.description: Give Claude Code or Codex consistent rules before changing a project.

use_cases.title: Document Delivery Expectations
use_cases.description: Turn testing, deployment, and environment requirements into a concise project reference.

prompt_templates.title: Explain the Project Rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to explain the project architecture, testing rules, and deployment workflow in plain language.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Feature Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the project guidelines to plan a feature across frontend, backend, tests, and deployment steps.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Pull Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this change against the skill guidelines. Focus on architecture, validation, tests, and production readiness.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt the Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this example skill into guidelines for my project stack. Preserve useful structure and replace stack-specific details.

output_examples.input: Plan a new workspace feature using the guidelines.
output_examples.output: A feature plan with frontend components, backend routes, shared models, required tests, and deployment checks.

output_examples.input: Review a backend change for compliance.
output: Validation is required for request data.
A pytest case should cover the success path and error path.
Deployment notes should mention any new environment variables.

output_examples.input: Adapt this template for another SaaS app.
output_examples.output: A revised guideline outline with architecture, file structure, coding patterns, testing rules, and release workflow.

best_practices: Replace all placeholder service names, URLs, and environment variables before use.
Keep the guideline aligned with the real repository structure.
Update testing and deployment commands when tooling changes.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the example architecture as universal for every project.
Do not paste real secrets into the environment variable section.
Do not rely on the template instead of running tests.

faq.question: Is this skill tied to one exact repository?
faq.answer: No. It is an example project guideline template that can be adapted.

faq.question: Does it run deployment commands?
faq.answer: No. It only documents example commands and checklist items.

faq.question: Does it include real API keys?
faq.answer: No. The environment values are placeholders and should be replaced securely.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What stack does it assume?
faq.answer: It assumes Next.js, TypeScript, FastAPI, Supabase, Claude API, Cloud Run, and common test tools.

faq.question: Can it enforce code quality automatically?
faq.answer: No. It guides AI output, but separate tests and linters must enforce quality.
